title: T003: Configure agenda-tui/Cargo.toml
description: Configure agenda-tui/Cargo.toml — ratatui, crossterm, agenda-core dependency

title: T017: Implement rule engine in engine.rs
description:
Implement rule engine in agenda-core/src/engine.rs: process_item() — depth-first walk of category hierarchy, evaluate conditions, fire actions (Assign + Remove). Takes a Store reference and a Classifier.

The engine evaluates two levels of auto-assignment:
1. ImplicitString (name-based, zero-config): check category.enable_implicit_string flag. If true, run Classifier against item text and category name. The bool flag is the source of truth — do NOT check the conditions vec for Condition::ImplicitString.
2. Profile (assignment-based, user-configured): check the conditions vec for Profile { criteria } entries. Evaluate as set-membership: item must be in ALL criteria.include categories AND NOT in ANY criteria.exclude categories. Ignore virtual_include/virtual_exclude/text_search (those are for View queries, Phase 4). Profile conditions create cascading rules — e.g. 'if in Urgent AND Project Alpha, assign to Escalated.'

On match: assign with source AutoMatch, fire actions. Assign actions add targets. Remove actions are collected but deferred (not applied mid-cascade). Existing assignments are skipped (sticky — engine never revokes).

See spec/prompts/t017-rule-engine.md for full implementer prompt. See spec/design-decisions.md §1-3 for design rationale.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 13:20. Plan: implement process_item single-pass rule engine with implicit/profile matching, assign/remove action handling, and focused unit tests.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Implemented single-pass process_item in engine.rs with depth-first hierarchy walk, implicit string + profile condition matching, sticky assignment semantics, action handling (Assign immediate, Remove deferred), and 8 focused engine tests.

title: T019: Implement subsumption in engine
description: Implement subsumption in engine: when assigning to child, create implicit assignments for all ancestors (source: Subsumption). Walk up the parent chain from the assigned category and create Assignment records with source=Subsumption for each ancestor. These ancestor assignments should not re-trigger condition evaluation (they are bookkeeping).

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 16:28. Plan: implement subsumption ancestor assignment hook in engine assign path; add tests for multi-level, stickiness, action non-firing, and no extra fixed-point churn.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Added subsumption to engine assignment flow in engine.rs: successful AutoMatch/Action assignments now create sticky ancestor assignments with source=Subsumption and origin=subsumption:child-name; existing assignments are preserved; subsumption does not fire ancestor actions and is excluded from pass new_assignments. Added tests for basic and multi-level subsumption, no-overwrite, no ancestor action firing, non-counting in pass result, and action-triggered subsumption.

title: T021: Implement retroactive assignment in engine
description: Implement retroactive assignment in engine: evaluate_all_items(category_id) — runs classifier against all items for a newly created/modified category. When a category is created or its conditions change, iterate over all items in the store and run process_item for the new/changed category. This is the mechanism that makes 'create a category named Sarah → all items containing Sarah get assigned' work.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Implementer prompt written: spec/prompts/t021-retroactive-assignment.md. Key points: evaluate_all_items runs process_item on every item in the store. O(items×categories) is fine for MVP. Error handling strategy is implementer's choice. Don't filter items — run all through the engine.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 17:01. Plan: implement retroactive assignment so parent-level matches/actions are re-applied to sibling state changes created by mutual exclusion; maintain fixed-point semantics and add focused engine tests.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Added public evaluate_all_items(store, classifier, category_id) in engine.rs with fail-fast error strategy. It validates category existence, iterates all items via store.list_items(), runs process_item per item, and returns aggregate counters (processed, affected, total new assignments, deferred removals). Added retroactive tests for basic match, no double assignment, actions, subsumption, mutual exclusion, empty store, and idempotent rerun.

title: T015: Define Classifier trait in matcher.rs

MVP: SubstringClassifier returns Some(1.0) or None. The trait preserves the hook for future LLM-based classification.

title: T022: Wire engine into store operations
description: Wire engine into store operations: item create/update triggers process_item, category create triggers evaluate_all_items. This is the integration point where the engine becomes automatic. Consider whether to add an Engine struct that wraps Store + Classifier, or add methods directly. The engine should run synchronously for MVP.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Implementer prompt written: spec/prompts/t022-wire-engine.md. Key points: Create an integration layer (Agenda/Engine/Core struct recommended) that holds Store + Classifier. Triggers: item create/update → process_item, category create/update → evaluate_all_items, manual assign → process_item for cascading. Synchronous for MVP.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Added agenda integration wrapper in agenda.rs: Agenda {store, classifier} now triggers engine synchronously for create_item, update_item, create_category, update_category, and assign_item_manual. Engine errors are propagated after store mutation, preserving data durability. Added integration tests for automatic classification on item/category mutations, manual-assignment cascades, engine-failure durability, and end-to-end action+subsumption flow.

title: T020: Implement mutual exclusion in engine
description: Implement mutual exclusion in engine: when assigning to child of exclusive parent, unassign from sibling children. If a category's parent has is_exclusive=true, assigning an item to that category must first unassign the item from all other children of the same parent. Only one child of an exclusive parent can hold an item at a time.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 16:41. Plan: enforce parent-based mutual exclusion before assignment persistence for AutoMatch and Action paths; unassign sibling children immediately; keep subsumption ordering intact; add engine.rs tests for exclusive/non-exclusive and source-agnostic behavior.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Implemented immediate mutual exclusion in engine assignment flow: before assigning a child, if its parent is exclusive, sibling child assignments are unassigned immediately from store and in-memory assignment state. Applied to both AutoMatch and Action assignment paths while preserving subsumption ordering. Added tests for basic exclusive switching, non-exclusive coexistence, engine-match exclusivity, action-path exclusivity, manual-source removal, and three-child sibling behavior.

title: T018: Implement fixed-point loop in engine
description:
Implement fixed-point loop in engine: wrap T017's single-pass logic in a loop that re-runs while new assignments are produced, with max 10 passes.

Key behaviors:
1. Loop re-runs hierarchy walk until a pass produces zero new assignments (fixed point).
2. Pass cap: if pass 10 still produces new assignments, return an error — this indicates a rule configuration bug.
3. Cycle detection: HashSet<(ItemId, CategoryId)> seen-set persists across passes. Already-seen pairs are skipped.
4. Deferred removals: Action::Remove targets accumulate during cascade, applied after loop exits. Removals do NOT trigger re-evaluation.
5. Remove regardless of source: Remove actions apply uniformly — Manual, AutoMatch, Action, Subsumption assignments are all removed (spec/design-decisions.md §4).
6. Atomicity (optional): if Store API allows, wrap in DB transaction for rollback on error. Skip if non-trivial.

See spec/prompts/t018-fixed-point-loop.md for full implementer prompt. See spec/design-decisions.md §3-5 for design rationale.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 15:10. Plan: implement fixed-point wrapper around T017 pass logic with seen-pair cycle guard, deferred remove application after convergence, 10-pass hard error, optional transaction if low-scope.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Implemented fixed-point process_item loop with up to 10 passes, persistent (ItemId, CategoryId) seen-set across passes, deferred Remove application after convergence, remove-regardless-of-source behavior, and transactional rollback on cap-exceeded errors. Added comprehensive engine tests for cascades, cycle convergence, cap error, deferred removes, and idempotent re-run.
